input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
with open('articles/2026/02/23/articles.jsonl') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
print(f'Total articles: {len(lines)}')
# Search for relevant topics
matches = []
for line in lines:
    a = json.loads(line)
    title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
    content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:1500]
    # Key searches
    checks = [
        ('mosque attack', 'mosque' in title and ('attack' in content or 'axe' in content or 'weapon' in content or 'arrest' in content)),
        ('congo grave', ('congo' in title or 'drc' in title) and ('grave' in content or 'mass' in content or 'bodies' in content)),
        ('spain friendly', 'spain' in title and ('friendly' in content or 'warm' in content or 'opponent' in content or 'match' in content)),
        ('t20 wc', 't20' in title and ('super' in content or 'result' in content or 'win' in content)),
        ('ucl draw', 'champions league' in title and ('draw' in content or 'round of 16' in content)),
        ('gorton', 'gorton' in title or 'denton' in title),
        ('iran talks', 'iran' in title and ('talk' in content or 'nuclear' in content or 'geneva' in content)),
        ('barcelona injury', 'barcelona' in title and ('injur' in content or 'hamstring' in content)),
        ('prediction market', 'prediction market' in title or ('senator' in title and 'ban' in content)),
        ('newcastle ucl', 'newcastle' in title and ('champions' in content or 'draw' in content)),
    ]
    for label, match in checks:
        if match:
            matches.append((label, (a.get('title','') or '')[:120], (a.get('content','') or '')[:300]))
            break
for label, title, content in matches[:30]:
    print(f'[{label}] {title}')
    print(f'  {content[:200]}')
    print()
" 2>/dev/null

blocks.content:
Starmer takes aim at rivals in battle for Labour to retain Gorton and Denton
Sir Keir Starmer hit out at the Green Party’s “disgusting” drugs policy and “toxic” Reform UK on a campaign visit ahead of the Gorton and Denton by-election.
Labour faces a battle to save the previously rock-solid Greater Manchester constituency in the face of a double electoral threat from both Nigel Farage’s Reform UK and Zack Polanski’s Greens.
In 2024 Labour won the seat with a majority of 13,413 and more than half the vote, but the party’s plummeting popularity since Sir Keir entered No 10

blocks.content:
Hetmyer runs rampage as West Indies crush Zimbabwe in T20 World Cup
Shimron Hetmyer scored a 19-ball half-century as his scorching knock of 85 led West Indies to a dominant 107-run win over Zimbabwe in their first Super Eights match at the Twenty20 World Cup, continuing the two-time champions' winning run.
Hetmyer scored the fastest fifty by a West Indian at a T20 World Cup as the two-time champions posted a mammoth total of 254-6 in Mumbai, the second-highest total in the tournament's history after Sri Lanka's 260-6 against Kenya in 2007.
Zimbabwe were all out for 147 as Gudakesh Motie took four wickets, sending two-time champions West Indies to the top of th
===

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Search for UCL R16 draw, Barcelona injury, Turkey collision, university ICE
with open('articles/2026/02/23/articles.jsonl') as f:
    for line in f:
        a = json.loads(line)
        title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
        content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:2000]
        if 'barcelona' in title and ('injur' in content or 'hamstring' in content or 'ruled out' in content):
            print(f'BARCA: {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {(a.get(\"content\",\"\") or \"\")[:400]}')
            print()
        elif ('champions league' in title and ('draw' in title or 'round of 16' in title or 'last 16' in title)):
            print(f'UCL: {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {(a.get(\"content\",\"\") or \"\")[:400]}')
            print()
        elif ('turkey' in title or 'turkish' in title) and ('migrant' in content[:500] or 'boat' in content[:500] or 'collision' in content[:500] or 'coastguard' in content[:500]):
            print(f'TURKEY: {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {(a.get(\"content\",\"\") or \"\")[:400]}')
            print()
        elif 'university' in title and ('ice' in content[:500] or 'immigration' in content[:500] or 'misrepresent' in content[:500]):
            print(f'UNIV: {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {(a.get(\"content\",\"\") or \"\")[:400]}')
            print()
" 2>/dev/null

  Players from the BU Terriers walked to practice in full uniform in the middle of a blizzard.

UNIV: Boston University hockey team walks to practice in full uniform during blizzard
  The Boston University men's hockey team didn't let a blizzard stop them from getting to practice on Monday.
With winds whipping and snow piling up, members of the Terriers made their way down the sidewalk on Comm. Ave. for practice while WBZ-TV's Penny Kmitt was reporting on the blizzard.
"Just headed to practice on a snowy Monday. It's pretty nice weather out," said junior defenseman Aiden Celebr
